skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X with posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI
skill.summary: Post tweets, like, retweet, send DMs, and follow users through the inference.sh platform with restricted bash commands
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 48 patterns but most are false positives from documentation. External command findings are restricted to infsh CLI via allowed-tools directive. Network URLs are documentation links only. Pipe-to-shell install pattern is standard with checksum verification. Low risk due to command restrictions and transparent documentation.

medium_findings.title: Pipe to Shell Installation Pattern
medium_findings.description: The installation command uses curl piped to sh (curl | sh). While this is a common installation pattern, it requires trust in the remote server. The documentation states checksums are verified, which mitigates risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirmed in documentation. Risk is mitigated by checksum verification mentioned in install notes, but still requires trusting the remote server during installation.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution via CLI
low_findings.description: The skill executes commands through the infsh CLI tool. However, the allowed-tools directive restricts bash to only infsh commands, limiting the attack surface.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools directive explicitly restricts to Bash(infsh *), which is a security control. Commands are documented usage examples for the CLI tool.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains External URLs
low_findings.description: Multiple external URLs to inference.sh domain are present in documentation. These are informational links, not active network calls in code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are clearly documentation links for reference. No evidence of unauthorized network access in the skill logic itself.

content.user_title: Automate Twitter and X with AI-powered posting
content.value_statement: Social media managers and developers waste time on repetitive Twitter tasks. This skill automates posting, engagement, and user management through the inference.sh CLI platform.
actual_capabilities: Post text tweets to Twitter/X via the x/post-tweet app
Create posts with media attachments using x/post-create
Like tweets and retweet content with x/post-like and x/post-retweet
Send direct messages through the x/dm-send app
Follow users and retrieve user profiles with x/user-follow and x/user-get
Delete tweets and retrieve tweet details by ID
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication before use
Depends on inference.sh platform availability and API rate limits
Cannot access Twitter API features not exposed through inference.sh apps
Media posting requires valid URLs accessible by the inference.sh platform

use_cases.title: Content Creator Scheduling
use_cases.description: Schedule and post content automatically at optimal times for audience engagement
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Engagement Automation
use_cases.description: Automatically like and retweet content from specific accounts or hashtags

use_cases.title: Developer Workflow Integration
use_cases.description: Post automated updates from CI/CD pipelines or application events

prompt_templates.title: Post a Simple Tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Post a tweet with the text: Hello from inference.sh!

prompt_templates.title: Post with Media
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a post with the text Check out this image and the media URL from the provided image file

prompt_templates.title: Engagement Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the latest tweet from username and like it, then follow the user if they have more than 1000 followers

prompt_templates.title: AI Content Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an AI image of a sunset over mountains, then post it to Twitter with an engaging caption about AI art

output_examples.input: Post a tweet saying Just shipped a new feature

output_examples.output: User: OpenAI - Followers: 5.2M - Verified account focused on AI safety and research

best_practices: Always review scheduled content before publishing to maintain brand voice consistency
Respect Twitter API rate limits by spacing out bulk operations
Use meaningful and descriptive text in tweets for better accessibility
anti_patterns: Do not automate follow-unfollow cycles as this violates Twitter automation rules
Avoid posting identical content repeatedly as this may trigger spam detection
Do not send unsolicited bulk DMs which can result in account suspension

faq.question: What do I need to start using this skill?
faq.answer: You need to install the inference.sh CLI from cli.inference.sh and authenticate with your account. Then you can run Twitter automation commands through Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with my Twitter account?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill uses the official inference.sh platform which connects to Twitter API. Your credentials are managed by inference.sh, not exposed to the AI assistant. Always follow Twitter automation rules.

faq.question: Can I post images and videos to Twitter?
faq.answer: Yes, use the x/post-create app which supports media attachments. Provide a publicly accessible URL for your image or video, or use integrated AI generation skills to create content first.

faq.question: How do I schedule tweets for later posting?
faq.answer: The current apps support immediate posting. For scheduling, you would need to integrate with a cron system or use the inference.sh platform scheduling features if available.

faq.question: What happens if a Twitter API call fails?
faq.answer: The inference.sh CLI will return an error message with details about the failure. Common causes include rate limits, invalid input, or authentication issues.

faq.question: Can this skill interact with other platforms besides Twitter?
faq.answer: This skill is Twitter-specific. However, inference.sh offers 150+ other apps for different platforms. You can combine skills for multi-platform workflows.
